# Getting started with brms

# Installation

`brms`'s power and flexibility require special preparations. First,
update R to its latest available version. This will ensure that all our
libraries will be compatible with each other. Installing brms with an older
version of R may work, but is likely to cause problems later.

## C++ toolchain

We have to set up our computer so that it can use C++ in the background. This
setup only needs to be done once.

### In Windows

For Windows, we need to install RTools 4.5 (available
[here](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/rtools45/rtools.html)).
Use the Rtools45 installer and follow its instructions, keeping all the default
choices.

### In macOS

For macOS, we need to install Xcode Command Line Tools. To check if this is
already installed, go to the Terminal (open `Finder > Applications >
Terminal`) and run

```
g++ --version
make --version
```

If your computer has `g++` at version 4.9.3 or later and `make` at version
3.81 or later, you do not need to install anything else (although newer
versions of `g++` are faster). If you do not have these tools, or if you want
to update them, run the commands:

```
sudo apt install g++
sudo apt install make
```

```{r install brms}
#| eval: false
install.packages("brms")
```

This installation includes several different packages, so it may take a few minutes to finish.

 The first
step is to install a special R library called `devtools`.
